name: Architecture
description: Architects, buildings, and styles from the Pantheon to the Shard.

questions.q: Who designed Barcelona's Sagrada Família, still under construction a century after his death?

source.notes: Catalan Modernist; began the basilica in 1882.

questions.q: The Guggenheim Museum in New York, with its famous spiral interior, was designed by which American architect?
In which Spanish city is Frank Gehry's famously wavy Guggenheim Museum located?
Who designed the iconic glass pyramid in the main courtyard of the Louvre?
The Sydney Opera House, with its sail-like shells, was designed by which Danish architect?
Which influential modernist design school, founded in 1919 by Walter Gropius, is based in Germany?

source.notes: Bauhaus closed in 1933 under Nazi pressure.

questions.q: Which style of architecture is marked by pointed arches, ribbed vaults, and flying buttresses?
Which English architect, known for St Paul's Cathedral, rebuilt much of London after the 1666 Great Fire?

source.notes: Designed 52 London churches after the fire.

questions.q: Fallingwater, a home cantilevered over a waterfall in Pennsylvania, was designed by whom?
The Dome of St. Peter's Basilica in Rome was primarily designed by which Renaissance artist?

source.notes: Michelangelo took over as chief architect in 1546.

questions.q: Which Brazilian modernist designed much of the planned capital city Brasília?

source.notes: Including the cathedral, congress buildings, and Palácio da Alvorada.

questions.q: Which Pritzker-winning Iraqi-British architect, known for sweeping futuristic curves, died in 2016?

source.notes: First woman to win the Pritzker Prize (2004).

questions.q: The Shard — London's tallest building — was designed by which Italian architect?

source.notes: Piano also co-designed the Centre Pompidou.

questions.q: The Walt Disney Concert Hall in Los Angeles is a stainless-steel-sheathed design by which architect?
The 'inside-out' Centre Pompidou in Paris, with exposed ducts and pipes, was co-designed by Renzo Piano and whom?

source.original_a: Renzo Piano and Richard Rogers (Piano & Rogers)

questions.q: Which architectural movement, popular in the 1950s-70s, features raw exposed concrete as its hallmark?
Which architect, born in Toronto, designed the undulating Walt Disney Concert Hall and the Bilbao Guggenheim?

source.notes: Gehry was born Frank Goldberg in Toronto, 1929.

questions.q: Which three classical Greek orders of columns are the foundation of Western architectural style?

source.notes: Tuscan and Composite were Roman additions.

questions.q: What mud-and-straw, sun-dried building material — whose name comes from an Arabic word — is common in the American Southwest?
Norman Foster's 'Gherkin' tower (30 St Mary Axe) is located in which UK city?
Which Finnish-American architect designed the Gateway Arch in St. Louis?
Le Corbusier coined which famous phrase to describe houses?

source.notes: 'Une machine à habiter' — from Vers une architecture, 1923.
